channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Protests erupt in Colombia as far-right set to win presidential election
item.description: Protests have erupted in Colombia against the election of the far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella. Though election officials have yet to formally announce a winner, with almost all of the votes counted, the pro-Trump lawyer and businessman is set to defeat his left-wing opponent Ivan Cepeda. Trailing behind by around 250 000 votes, the left-wing senator told supporters his team would be challenging results from tens of thousands of voting stations.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran set to return to mainstream oil market as US temporarily waives sanctions
item.description: As 60 days of peace negotiations between the US and Iran continue, Tehran appears to have won significant concessions from Washington. Chief among those is the waiving of sanctions on Iranian oil exports. US sanctions have been in place for decades, cutting Iran off from mainstream global oil markets and forcing it to sell its oil to China at discount rates. The US says the other side of the deal is that Iran will allow international inspectors access to its nuclear program - Tehran though says it has made no such commitment.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Messi becomes World Cup all-time goal scorer in game against Austria
item.description: France's second World Cup game belonged to Mbappé as he scored twice, moving into a tie for second in World Cup goals. Argentina won its second game against Austria, with two goals scored by Messi. Norway's Haaland also scored two goals in the game against Senegal, whose fate now depends on its game against Iraq. FRANCE 24's James Vasina tells us what to know.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France faces historically hot temperatures as heatwave carries on
item.description: Millions of people across France woke up drenched in sweat on June 23 after another night of scorching heat, with most of the population exposed to extreme and exceptional temperatures. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, schools, trains and sporting events remain impacted, while some 20 drowning deaths have been reported since the weekend. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from the Eiffel Tower in Paris.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK facing 'pivotal moment' as it prepares for 7th PM since Brexit vote
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer won his job after leading his party to a massive victory in 2024. Less than two years later, he resigned as his party rebelled in the wake of widespread losses in local elections. It coincides with the tenth anniversary of the Brexit vote, which led to the resignation of Conservative Prime Minister David Cameron. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot and Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi look at how the UK's relationship with the EU will weigh on Starmer's successor, and what led Starmer to resignation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US, Iran agree on Lebanon ceasefire and lifting sanctions on Iranian oil, but much remains
item.description: Both Tehran and Washington hailed the negotiations that took place in Switzerland on June 22 to create a roadmap to close down the war in the Middle East. US Vice president JD Vance said it laid a good foundation for a deal to end the war. The parties agreed to establish mechanisms to open the strait of Hormuz but it is unclear who will control it.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: England: Lawn Mower Championships: 'Proper British fun'
item.description: Dozens of speed demons are competing at the British Lawn Mower Championships in Chichester, southern England.These mowers have been modified and have had their blades removed for optimal speed. They can reach an impressive 55mph (88kmph) at top speed. While often seen as a fun motorsport, the racing is incredibly physical.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US waives Iran oil sanctions as part of 60-day peace negotiations
item.description: The United States temporarily waived sanctions on Iranian oil exports on Monday, opening the door for Tehran to return to global energy markets as negotiations between the two countries continue. In this edition, we look at the impact this has had on oil prices, while also examining major job cuts at Oracle linked to AI-driven restructuring and a sharp sell-off in technology stocks that's rippled from Wall Street to Asian and European markets.
item.dc:creator: Charles PELLEGRIN

item.title: Israel’s targeting of Palestinian children establishes ‘genocidal intent’ in Gaza, UN probe says
item.description: Israeli authorities and security forces "deliberately targeted and killed" children in what has become a key factor in its "genocidal intent" to "destroy the larger Palestinian group in Gaza", a UN inquiry revealed Tuesday. Israel has slammed the report as "defamatory" and a "libellous sham".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: France into last 32 with win over Iraq despite two-hour weather delay
item.description: With a second win in two matches, Les Bleus secured their place in the World Cup round of 32. Although they were never really troubled by Iraq (3-0), France were mainly disrupted by a storm over Philadelphia, which suspended the match for almost two hours between the first and second halves.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Afghan Taliban set for EU migration talks slammed by rights groups
item.description: The EU is expected to hold talks on returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers with a Taliban delegation in Brussels Tuesday after Belgium issued one-day visas for the team, overriding criticisms from human rights groups. The 27-member bloc does not recognise the Taliban administration, but the European Commission invited a team from Kabul for discussions amid a push to boost deportations.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Lebanon war damage estimated at $1.3 billion, but who will foot the bill?
item.description: Massive destruction in southern Lebanon in the latest Israel-Hezbollah war is estimated at around $1.38 billion, according to a UN agency and a Lebanese research centre. Reporting from Beirut, FRANCE 24’s Renée Davis notes that while the US-Iran deal includes a proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran, Lebanon’s reconstruction was not mentioned in the 14-point agreement.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Algeria beats Jordan 2-1 to clinch its first World Cup win since 2014
item.description: Algeria stormed back to beat Jordan 2-1 to advance to the 2026 World Cup knockout round in California late Monday thanks to second-half goals from substitute Nadhir Benbouali and Amine Gouiri. It marked the first time Algeria won a World Cup game since 2014.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 'Significant damage': How Brexit is still draining the UK economy 10 years later
item.description: Ten years after Brexit, data shows that the decision to leave the European Union has made people in Britain up to 8 percent poorer. Experts predict the economic damage is set to continue, and YouGov polling now finds nearly 60 percent of Britons say it was wrong to leave the EU.
item.dc:creator: Joanna YORK

item.title: World Cup 2026: Haaland strikes twice as Norway secure knockout progression
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Haaland strikes twice as Norway secure knockout progression
item.description: Norway survived a Senegal onslaught to claim a crucial 3-2 victory in Group I, with Erling Haaland scoring twice to secure their qualification to advance to the knockout stage. The Africans fought back from 2-0 down with a goal from Ismaila Sarr before mounting a desperate late assault, but Norway held firm.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Iran says no visit scheduled for UN nuclear inspectors
item.description: Iran on Tuesday said there were no visits scheduled for inspectors from the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) to see nuclear sites bombed by the US. The Iranian foreign ministry spokesman's remarks appeared to undercut those made by US Vice President JD Vance, who said negotiations in Switzerland won an agreement for an IAEA visit to the sites. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: France-Iraq match disrupted amid thunderstorm
item.description: Though France's "Les Bleus" won their group stage fixture against Iraq in commanding fashion on Monday, the two sides were forced to stop play for nearly two hours due to a thunderstorm hitting the venue they were playing at in Philadelphia.What happened, and why was play stopped? FRANCE 24 explains.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé stars as France breeze past Iraq despite weather chaos
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé stars as France breeze past Iraq despite weather chaos
item.description: France defeated Iraq 3-0 in Philadelphia to mathematically secure their passage to the knockout stages. Kylian Mbappé scored twice and Ousmane Dembélé added a third, but the match was overshadowed by severe thunderstorms that forced a two hour and 12-minute delay, the evacuation of spectators and a complete washout of the pitch.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Romania parliament rejects liberal PM-designate Adrian Vestea
item.description: Romania's parliament rejected prime minister-designate Adrian Vestea on Monday, extending months of political turmoil in the EU and NATO member bordering Ukraine and raising the prospect of a snap election if a second government nominee also fails to secure lawmakers' backing.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: France-Iraq match suspended as stadium is evacuated due to thunderstorms
item.description: Heavy rain and an approaching thunderstorm delayed the second half of France's World Cup match against Iraq on Monday, forcing thousands of fans to seek shelter in the first weather-related disruption of the tournament. The stoppage came as severe weather also threatened another fixture in nearby New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Vance says UN nuclear inspectors will return to Iran as US suspends sanctions
item.description: The United States temporarily eased sanctions on Iranian oil on Monday after Vice President JD Vance said Tehran had agreed to allow UN nuclear inspectors back into the country, marking a significant step in renewed negotiations aimed at curbing Iran's nuclear programme and stabilising the Strait of Hormuz.
item.source: © Urs Flueeler, Pool Photo via AP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Rubio heads to UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain to discuss Iran deal with Gulf allies
item.description: Starting Tuesday, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is slated to visit three Gulf allies that were attacked by Iran during the US and Israeli war: the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Bahrain. Rubio appears to have his work cut out for him. The US’s Gulf allies were not warned in advance of “Operation Epic Fury” and now, many of them are being asked to invest in Iran’s reconstruction. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
